RESPONSE TO MTN TO QUASH SUMMOSN AND MTN TO STAY FILED BY PLAINTIFF LEGALMATCH.COM

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL VAN CLEVE'S MOTION TO QUASH SUMMONS AND MOTION TO STAY - THE MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED. "WHEN A NONRESIDENT DEFENDANT CHALLENGES PERSONAL JURISDICTION THE BURDEN SHIFTS TO THE PLAINTIFF TO DEMONSTRATE BY A PREPONDERANCE OF THE EVIDENCE THAT ALL NECESSARY JURISDICTIONAL CRITERIA ARE MET. [CITATION] THIS BURDEN MUST BE MET BY COMPETENT EVIDENCE IN AFFIDAVITS AND AUTHENTICATED DOCUMENTARY EVIDENCE." ZILLER ELECTRONICS LAB GMBH V. SUP. CRT. (1988) 206 CAL.APP.3D 1222, 1232. HERE, PLAINTIFF ARGUES THAT DEFENDANT CONSENTED TO CALIFORNIA JURISDICTION BY ENTERING INTO THE ATTORNEY MEMBERSHIP AGREEMENT. IN OTHER WORDS, PLAINTIFF ARGUES THAT DEFENDANT CONSENTED TO CALIFORNIA JURISDICTION. THE ONLY COMPETENT EVIDENCE BEFORE THE COURT DEMONSTRATES THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT CONSENT TO CALIFORNIA JURISDICTION. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN OF PROOF. THE ALTERNATIVE RELIEF SOUGHT (A MOTION TO STAY) IS MOOT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Off Calendar Mar-05-2014 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Quash Summons And Motion To Stay
Off Calendar Apr-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - MICHAEL VAN CLEVE, TEL. NO. (334) 233-8842, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MICHAEL VAN CLEVE'S Motion To Quash Summons And Motion To Stay - The motion to quash is granted. "When a nonresident defendant challenges personal jurisdiction the burden shifts to the plaintiff to demonstrate by a preponderance of the evidence that all necessary jurisdictional criteria are met. [citation] This burden must be met by competent evidence in affidavits and authenticated documentary evidence." Ziller Electronics Lab GmbH v. Sup. Crt. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 1222, 1232. Here, plaintiff argues that defendant consented to California jurisdiction by entering into the Attorney Membership Agreement. In other words, plaintiff argues that defendant consented to California jurisdiction. The only competent evidence before the Court demonstrates that defendant did not consent to California jurisdiction. Plaintiff has failed to meet its burden of proof. The alternative relief sought (a motion to stay) is moot. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
